content.problemStatement:
Pantelija was given a sequence of positive integers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1‚Äč,a2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,aN‚Äč. Let's define the gcd value of this sequence as the number of its non-empty contiguous subsequences with greatest common divisor strictly greater than 111. The greatest common divisor of any contiguous subsequence al,al+1,‚Ä¶,ara_l, a_{l+1}, \ldots, a_ral‚Äč,al+1‚Äč,‚Ä¶,ar‚Äč (1‚Č§l‚Č§r‚Č§N1 \le l \le r \le N1‚Č§l‚Č§r‚Č§N) is the greatest positive integer which divides each element of this subsequence.

Pantelija wants to maximise the gcd value of this sequence. In order to do that, he may choose a valid index iii, an integer bbb (1‚Č§b‚Č§5‚čÖ1051 \le b \le 5 \cdot 10^51‚Č§b‚Č§5‚čÖ105, since he does not like large numbers) and change the element aia_iai‚Äč to bbb. What is the maximum possible gcd value he can obtain?
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1‚Äč,a2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,aN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer ‚Äē the largest possible gcd value.
